name: the-book-of-basketball
description: Bill Simmons' magnum opus on NBA history — the Hall of Fame Pyramid ranking the top 96 players, The Secret of winning basketball, the What-If Game, the Wine Cellar of greatest moments, and why chemistry matters more than stats.
useCase: Understanding NBA history, debating all-time player rankings, learning what makes championship teams, appreciating basketball's greatest moments and what-ifs.
knownRisks: Written from a passionate Celtics fan perspective — Simmons openly biases toward Bird/Celtics history
Opinions are deliberately provocative to spark arguments — not a neutral reference work
Data and rankings are as of 2009 — LeBron's prime, Curry's revolution, and modern NBA are not covered
Heavy on Boston-centric storytelling (Prologue is pure Celtics nostalgia)
Extensive footnotes and digressions make it more entertainment than reference
